001package edu.cnm.deepdive.fossilsweeper.controller;
002
003import android.os.Bundle;
004import android.view.LayoutInflater;
005import android.view.View;
006import android.view.ViewGroup;
007import androidx.annotation.NonNull;
008import androidx.annotation.Nullable;
009import androidx.fragment.app.Fragment;
010import androidx.navigation.Navigation;
011import dagger.hilt.android.AndroidEntryPoint;
012import edu.cnm.deepdive.fossilsweeper.databinding.FragmentFossilGalleryBinding;
013
014/**
015 * !!! Work in progress !!!
016 * Fragment displaying a gallery of collected fossils. Allows users to browse their fossil
017 * collection and view detailed information about individual specimens.
018 */
019@AndroidEntryPoint
020public class FossilGalleryFragment extends Fragment {
021
022  private FragmentFossilGalleryBinding binding;
023
024  @Nullable
025  @Override
026  public View onCreateView(@NonNull LayoutInflater inflater, @Nullable ViewGroup container,
027      @Nullable Bundle savedInstanceState) {
028    binding = FragmentFossilGalleryBinding.inflate(inflater, container, false);
029    // Set up navigation to MainFragment
030    binding.backButton.setOnClickListener(v ->
031        Navigation.findNavController(binding.getRoot())
032            .navigate(FossilGalleryFragmentDirections.navigateToMainFragment()));
033    // Set up test button for fossil dialog (with example fossil ID)
034    binding.testFossilDialogButton.setOnClickListener(v ->
035        Navigation.findNavController(binding.getRoot())
036            .navigate(FossilGalleryFragmentDirections.navigateToFossilViewDialog(2L)));
037    return binding.getRoot();
038  }
039
040  @Override
041  public void onViewCreated(@NonNull View view, @Nullable Bundle savedInstanceState) {
042    super.onViewCreated(view, savedInstanceState);
043    // TODO: Set up ViewModel and observers
044  }
045
046  @Override
047  public void onDestroyView() {
048    binding = null;
049    super.onDestroyView();
050  }
051}
